sleep_onset.title: Sleep onset
sleep_onset.description: Trouble getting sleepy or shifting into bedtime.

night_waking.title: Night waking
night_waking.description: Waking during the night or struggling to return to sleep.

racing_mind.title: Racing mind
racing_mind.description: Mental noise, tension, or bedtime rumination.

relaxation.title: Relaxation
relaxation.description: A gentler wind-down target before stronger sedating approaches.

claims.claim_statement: Melatonin has the clearest fit when the sleep problem is circadian timing or difficulty falling asleep.
claims.evidence_summary: Human syntheses report modest improvements in sleep latency and total sleep time, with the strongest practical fit around timing-related sleep onset.
claims.limitations: Effects are usually modest, dose and timing matter, and study populations do not represent every insomnia pattern.
claims.best_fit: Short-term schedule drift, travel-related rhythm disruption, or delayed sleep timing.
claims.not_best_fit: Unexplained daytime sleepiness, suspected sleep apnea, or chronic insomnia without clinician input.

claims.claim_statement: Magnesium may be most decision-useful when poor sleep overlaps with low intake context, tension, or older-adult sleep quality concerns.
claims.evidence_summary: Small human trials suggest possible sleep-quality benefits, but the evidence base is narrower than common supplement marketing implies.
claims.limitations: Benefits may depend on baseline magnesium status, age, form, and gastrointestinal tolerance.
claims.best_fit: A conservative evening routine where muscle tension or low dietary intake may be relevant.
claims.not_best_fit: Kidney disease, unexplained severe sleep symptoms, or a need for an immediate sedating effect.

claims.claim_statement: L-theanine is a plausible first-pass option when the barrier to sleep is mental tension rather than circadian timing.
claims.evidence_summary: Human research is more developed around stress and relaxation than direct sleep outcomes, but some studies include sleep-quality signals.
claims.limitations: Sleep-specific evidence is limited, effects can feel subtle, and results may not generalize to severe insomnia.
claims.best_fit: Bedtime rumination, mild tension, or a calm-down routine where next-day heaviness is a concern.
claims.not_best_fit: Frequent night waking, suspected medical sleep disorders, or situations involving sedatives or alcohol.

claims.claim_statement: Glycine may be worth researching when the goal is subjective sleep quality without a strongly sedating approach.
claims.evidence_summary: Small human studies suggest sleep-quality and next-day performance signals, but the evidence remains early and narrow.
claims.limitations: The research base is small, outcomes are often subjective, and product use should not distract from sleep hygiene or medical evaluation.
claims.best_fit: A simple non-botanical option for sleep quality when timing is not the main problem.
claims.not_best_fit: Severe insomnia, complex medical conditions, or expectations of a strong acute sedative effect.

claims.claim_statement: Lemon balm is best framed as a gentle relaxation candidate rather than a high-certainty sleep intervention.
claims.evidence_summary: Human evidence includes relaxation and mood-related signals, but sleep-specific evidence is less direct and product preparations vary.
claims.limitations: Ingredient form, extract standardization, blend context, and small study designs limit confidence.
claims.best_fit: Mild bedtime restlessness where a gentle botanical approach is preferred.
claims.not_best_fit: Medication-heavy contexts, pregnancy or breastfeeding, or a need for strong sleep-onset evidence.

safetyNotes.warning: Complex medical situations still deserve clinician guidance even when an ingredient appears low intensity.
safetyNotes.decision_effect: Keep glycine framed as a research option, not a substitute for evaluation of persistent sleep problems.

safetyNotes.warning: Low blood pressure or blood-pressure medication can change the risk calculation for relaxing compounds.
safetyNotes.decision_effect: Review medication context before using it as a bedtime relaxation option.

safetyNotes.warning: Combining relaxing botanicals with alcohol, sedatives, or multi-ingredient sleep blends can increase unwanted sedation.
safetyNotes.decision_effect: Avoid starting lemon balm as part of a large sleep stack.

safetyNotes.warning: Kidney disease changes magnesium handling and can make supplementation unsafe without clinician guidance.
safetyNotes.decision_effect: Do not use magnesium as a self-directed sleep experiment with kidney disease.

safetyNotes.warning: Pregnancy, breastfeeding, seizure history, autoimmune conditions, and pediatric use require clinician guidance.
safetyNotes.decision_effect: Pause self-directed use when any complex condition applies.

safetyNotes.warning: Morning grogginess, vivid dreams, or timing mismatch can occur, especially when dose or timing is poorly matched.
safetyNotes.decision_effect: Use timing and caution context before considering it as a sleep-onset experiment.

melatonin-sleep-onset.title: Melatonin in primary sleep disorders meta-analysis
melatonin-sleep-onset.source_note: Human synthesis reporting modest sleep latency and duration effects.

magnesium-sleep-quality.title: Magnesium supplementation and insomnia measures in older adults
magnesium-sleep-quality.source_note: Small human trial in older adults; useful but narrow population context.

l-theanine-racing-mind.title: L-theanine, stress-related symptoms, cognition, and sleep-quality signals
l-theanine-racing-mind.source_note: Human study with stress and sleep-quality measures; not a dedicated insomnia study.

glycine-sleep-quality.title: Glycine ingestion and subjective sleep quality in human volunteers
glycine-sleep-quality.source_note: Small human study focused on subjective sleep quality.

lemon-balm-relaxation.title: Melissa officinalis preparation and calmness-related outcomes
lemon-balm-relaxation.source_note: Human relaxation signal; sleep-specific inference should remain cautious.
